name: write_seq_read_unique_key
reason: Tests that a single-element write_seq correctly stores the value — basic sanity check of the lemma

name: write_seq_read_requires_uniqueness
code:
proof fn phi_2_write_seq_read_requires_uniqueness(pt: PTMem, addr: usize, v1: usize, v2: usize)
    requires
        v1 != v2,
    ensures
        pt.write_seq(seq![(addr, v1), (addr, v2)]).mem[addr] == v2,
{
}
reason: With duplicate keys, `lemma_write_seq_read` can't be applied (uniqueness precondition fails) — but fold_left still gives last-write-wins, testing that the spec is consistent even without the lemma

name: write_seq_read_does_not_require_domain
reason: `lemma_write_seq_read` has no precondition requiring the address to be in the original domain — writes can target arbitrary addresses outside allocated page table memory

name: write_seq_drop_last_preserves_earlier
reason: Earlier writes to distinct addresses survive later writes — tests that the inductive case correctly preserves non-overwritten entries

name: write_seq_empty_no_effect
reason: Empty write_seq is identity — tests fold_left base case
